tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine), and urinalysis shows significant proteinuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible renal pathology (e.g., nephrotic syndrome, acute kidney injury). Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, indicating possible hypoglycemia or malnutrition. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further evaluation of renal function, nutritional status, and possible underlying causes for proteinuria and ketonuria.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
